title: Allium abramsii
what_description: Allium abramsii is a rare perennial plant endemic to the southwestern United States. It belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae, a family of flowering plants previously known as Liliaceae. Botanically, Allium abramsii has strap-shaped leaves that are 2-8 cm long and 2-6 mm wide, and a scape that is 15-60 cm tall. The plant produces a spherical umbel of 10-30 white flowers, each with 6 tepals that are 4-6 mm long, and 6 stamens that are 2-3 mm long. The flowers bloom in late spring and early summer, and are pollinated by insects.

intro: Allium abramsii, also known as Abrams' onion, is a perennial plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and small, star-shaped flowers. The health benefits of Allium abramsii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as respiratory infections and wounds.
